title: Page-permissions grid + role rename + page-access enforcement
scope:
Builds the gadmin-only page-permissions grid at /user-management/permissions — a page × role matrix with instant-save toggles. Surfaces every system page on the rows; surfaces every editable role on the columns; gadmin column omitted (universal access enforced server-side); avatar-bucket pages excluded (locked floor enforced server-side).

Cannot ship in isolation. The current architecture has three structural problems the grid would expose without solving:

  1. Schema: users_roles_pages is keyed by the legacy user_role Postgres enum (3 values: gadmin/padmin/user). Tenant-custom roles cannot be granted pages through it. The grid needs UUID-keyed grants.
  2. Naming: 5 system role rows have rank-encoded UUIDs (e.g. 00000000-0000-0000-0000-00000000ad30 = gadmin/rank30) that lie about ranks the moment any rerank happens. Codes are inconsistent (mix of single-word + snake_case). Labels don't match the intended product hierarchy.
  3. Enforcement: there is NO per-route page-access middleware. Pages render based on rail visibility (cached 60s); access is only checked via permission codes that don't perfectly align with page grants. A user hand-typing a URL or hitting a stale bookmark renders the page; some API calls succeed, some 403 inconsistently.

Plan is structured as 5 phases with hard stop gates between every phase:

  • Phase 0 — Role schema cutover: retire 5 rank-encoded UUIDs, mint 7 new system roles (random UUIDs, snake_case grp_* codes, new labels, new ranks 10/20/30/40/50/60/70). Re-key users_roles_pages from enum to id_role UUID FK. Rewrite 12 string-comparing role gates in production Go to UUID compares. Rename 5 permission codes. Update 17 files / 79 lines per audit findings.
  • Phase 0.5 — Page-access enforcement: new pages_access_version integer + trigger; new RequirePageAccess(key_enum) middleware (server backstops); new usePageAccess hook (frontend UX). Stale-while-revalidate caching with 1s in-process version cache. Mount on the 19 page-bound route groups identified by the main.go audit. Add hook to the 34 mapped pages. File a tech-debt entry for the 41 unmapped/legacy pages.
  • Phase 1 — Grid CRUD: rebuild the grid backend with UUID-keyed shape. Frontend dynamic role columns + tri-state bucket-row checkbox + exclude avatar bucket. Server batch endpoint for bucket toggles. Server enforces avatar floor on writes (rejects DELETE attempts). Audit-log entry per write.
  • Phase 1.5 — Mid-session UX: in-place denial card on 403 (URL stays); nav rail polls + on-tab-focus re-checks pages_access_version; toast on access change.
  • Phase 2 — "You edit, we lock": after gadmin finishes editing the grid in Phase 1, capture the resulting users_roles_pages state for the 6 editable system roles into a replace-all seed migration that becomes the canonical defaults.

Audit findings (4 parallel Sonnet agents, 2026-05-14):

  • UUID retirement: 79 lines / 17 files need rewriting (5 production Go, 9 test Go, 65 SQL migrations).
  • Pages: 34 mapped / 41 unmapped / 5 dynamic. 20 of the unmapped are legacy /workspace-settings/** filesystem duplicates orphaned by mig 157 — these should be deleted as a cleanup task.
  • Route groups: 31 total. 19 page-bound and gateable; 12 cross-cutting/skip. Two architectural splits needed: composite /admin group, dual-purpose /topology.
  • Role gates: 12 production sites doing string-compare; concentrated in topology/ portfoliomodels/ portfolio/ fields/. Plus 5 type-narrowing switch arms in users/handler.go.
value:
Three product wins:

  1. Real role administration. A gadmin can create role types via /admin/roles, then immediately grant/revoke page access to those roles from one screen. Today the grid is a placeholder; tenant-created roles cannot even be granted pages.
  2. Honest enforcement. Page access becomes a server-enforced invariant. Hand-typing a URL or clicking a stale bookmark for a revoked page is denied at the API layer with a clean denial card, not partial 403s on data fetches. Closes a security gap that exists today.
  3. Stop-the-bleeding on naming. The 7 new system roles use clean codes (grp_*) and labels (Global Admin / Portfolio Manager / Product Owner / Team Lead / Team Member / Stakeholder / External). The rank-encoded UUID footgun is removed permanently.

Three architectural wins:

  1. The users_roles_pages table can finally hold tenant-custom roles — unblocks the entire "tenants build their own role model" feature area.
  2. The RequirePageAccess middleware becomes the canonical enforcement layer for any future page-bound resource. Adding a new page becomes "add row to pages, mount middleware on its route group" — no more ad-hoc gates scattered across handlers.
  3. The 12 string-comparing role gates in production Go are removed. Combined with the existing lint:role-literals, the exempt list shrinks toward zero.

Removes the page-permissions screen as a known gap and the role-code naming drift as a known mental-model bug. Lets you give a tenant a custom role and demo it end-to-end inside one session.
implementation_plan: Phase 0 — Role schema cutover (own commit, ~1 day). 0.1 Migration: insert 7 new users_roles rows with random UUIDs (grp_global/portfolio/product/team_lead/team_member/stakeholder/external) at ranks 70/60/50/40/30/20/10. 0.2 Migration: re-point users.role_id, users_roles_pages, role_permissions from old UUIDs to new via UPDATE...JOIN map; DELETE old rows. 0.3 Migration: drop users_roles_pages.role_enum column; rebuild PK as (id_page, id_role); update 5 SQL constants in nav/sql.go. 0.4 Migration: rename 5 permission codes (users.create.gadmin → users.create.grp_global etc.) + insert 2 new (grp_product, grp_stakeholder). 0.5 Update reservedSystemRanks in roles/service.go to {10,20,30,40,50,60,70}. 0.6 Update roles/service.go SystemRole* constants. 0.7 Rewrite 12 string-comparing role gates (topology/service.go x3, topology/commands.go x2, topology/permissions.go x1, portfoliomodels/handler_workspace_layers.go x2, portfolio/master_record_service.go x2, fields/service.go x1, app/(user)/user-management/permissions/page.tsx x1) to UUID compares. 0.8 Update 5 type-narrowing switch arms in users/handler.go. 0.9 Update Bookmarks.Pin to grant only actor's role (not all 3 enum values). 0.10 Update CatalogEntry.Roles type from []roletypes.Role to []uuid.UUID. 0.11 Tests: protected_accounts_test, roles service, nav registry, handler_creator_matrix. Stop gate: full test suite + smoke nav + smoke /admin/roles before Phase 0.5.

Phase 0.5 — Page-access enforcement (own commit, ~2-3 days). 0.5.1 Migration: pages_access_version singleton table with one integer; AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE trigger on users_roles_pages and users_roles bumps it. 0.5.2 New auth.RequirePageAccess(key_enum) middleware in backend/internal/auth/middleware.go. Reads version (1s in-process cache); on user-session version mismatch, fetches user's full page-access set from users_roles_pages JOIN users via role_id; caches per-user. 0.5.3 New /api/me/page-access endpoint OR fold into /api/nav/catalogue response (return access set + current version). 0.5.4 Frontend usePageAccess(key_enum) hook in app/contexts/AuthContext.tsx. 0.5.5 Mount middleware on 19 page-bound route groups in main.go (per audit: /admin/page-grants, /admin/* split into 3 child groups, /library/releases, /timeboxes/sprints, /timeboxes/releases, /portfolio site, /workspaces/{id}/portfolio site, /flows site, /flow-states site, /flow-state-exit-rules site, /workspaces/{id}/fields site, /work-items site+v2, /portfolio-items site+v2, /topology split into 2 child groups, /roles site, /artefact-types site, /portfolio-models site, /workspace-settings site, plus /samantha/v2 mirrors). 0.5.6 Add usePageAccess hook to 34 mapped pages in app/(user)/**. 0.5.7 Add 403 denial card primitive (PageAccessDenied component). 0.5.8 Decide-and-defer for 7 cross-cutting resource groups (/nav, /workspaces, /custom-pages, /me, /errors, /samantha/v2/search, /rank) — file as TD-PG-001. 0.5.9 Cleanup task: delete 20 legacy /workspace-settings/** filesystem-duplicate page.tsx files (orphaned by mig 157). 0.5.10 Tests: bookmark-click-after-revoke integration test, version-bump propagation across instances (stub for future LISTEN/NOTIFY). Stop gate: integration tests + smoke 5 randomly-sampled pages with revoke-then-bookmark scenario.

Phase 1 — Grid CRUD (own commit, ~1-2 days). 1.1 Backend rewrite of nav/admin_grants.go: GET /admin/page-grants returns roles as [{id, code, label, rank}] arrays (UUIDs); per-cell PUT/DELETE accept role_id (UUID), not role enum. Refuses gadmin role_id and avatar-bucket page_ids on writes. 1.2 New batch endpoint PUT /admin/page-grants/bucket/{tag_enum}/{role_id} with body {checked: bool} for tri-state bucket-row toggles; resolves to all pages in bucket (excluding avatar) and grants/revokes atomically. 1.3 GET /admin/page-grants/roles returns the editable role set (system non-gadmin + tenant, filtered by actor's subscription, ordered system-block-rank-desc then tenant-block-rank-desc). 1.4 Frontend: rebuild app/(user)/user-management/permissions/page.tsx to render dynamic role columns from /roles endpoint, exclude avatar-bucket rows entirely, render bucket-row checkboxes with native indeterminate state. 1.5 Tri-state cycling: indeterminate → all-on → all-off. 1.6 Server invariant: avatar-bucket DELETE returns 409 with usermessages.ResourceLocked. 1.7 Audit logging: every PUT/DELETE on grid → audit.Logger.Log({actor, action: 'grant'|'revoke', page_id, role_id}). 1.8 New role created via /admin/roles → backend immediately inserts users_roles_pages rows for every page in tag_enum='avatar_menu' with the new role_id. 1.9 Tests: grid CRUD, batch endpoint, avatar floor enforcement, audit-log entries. Stop gate: visual smoke + audit log spot-check.

Phase 1.5 — Mid-session UX (own commit, ~half day). 1.5.1 PageAccessDenied card component (uses denied page's title from registry; offers Back / Dashboard buttons). 1.5.2 usePageAccess hook returns {allowed: bool, loading: bool}; on allowed=false, page renders <PageAccessDenied /> in place of normal content (URL stays). 1.5.3 Nav polling: extend existing apiSite('/nav/catalogue') response to include current pages_access_version; client compares to cached version; on mismatch, evicts nav cache + re-fetches. 1.5.4 On tab focus (visibilitychange listener), fire one nav-version check. 1.5.5 Toast on access change: 'Your access changed. Some pages may now be unavailable.' (one toast per nav-version bump, debounced 5s). 1.5.6 Tests: simulate revoke during open session, assert page swaps to denial card on next API call.

Phase 2 — "You edit, we lock" seed migration (own commit, ~30 min after gadmin finishes editing in Phase 1). 2.1 Capture current users_roles_pages state for the 6 editable system roles (grp_portfolio, grp_product, grp_team_lead, grp_team_member, grp_stakeholder + grp_global universal seed). 2.2 Write replace-all migration: BEGIN; DELETE FROM users_roles_pages WHERE id_role IN (6 system UUIDs); INSERT … VALUES …; COMMIT. 2.3 Update protected_accounts_test to expect the locked grants. 2.4 Update docs/c_c_roles_permissions.md with the captured grant matrix table.

acceptance_criteria: Phase 0: full backend test suite passes; smoke /admin/roles renders 7 new labels; smoke nav rail renders for each of the 5 protected accounts; protected_accounts_test passes (5 human accounts unaltered).

Phase 0.5: integration test 'bookmark-click-after-revoke' passes — revoke a page from a user's role, simulate them clicking a bookmark to that page, assert 403 from the page's data API + denial card renders.
Phase 0.5: smoke test — sample 5 random pages from the 34 mapped set, hand-type URL as a non-granted user, observe denial card.
Phase 0.5: 20 legacy /workspace-settings/** orphan pages are deleted (filesystem and any nav rows).
Phase 1: gadmin can toggle a per-cell checkbox on the grid; toggle fires PUT or DELETE; UI flips optimistically; on error, reverts + toast.
Phase 1: gadmin can toggle a bucket-row checkbox; tri-state cycles indeterminate → all-on → all-off; server batch endpoint handles atomically.
Phase 1: avatar-bucket pages do NOT appear in the grid; server rejects DELETE on avatar pages with 409.

Phase 1: creating a new role via /admin/roles auto-seeds avatar-bucket grants; new role's column appears in grid on next load with avatar floor visible (or excluded since avatar excluded — assert the role can navigate to /preferences/navigation immediately).
Phase 1: every grid PUT/DELETE creates an audit-log entry with {actor_id, action, page_id, role_id}.
Phase 1.5: revoking a page during an open session causes the user's next API call from that page to return 403 + denial card swaps in (no redirect, URL preserved).
Phase 1.5: nav rail re-renders within 1s of page-grants version bump on tab focus; toast appears once per change.
Phase 2: replace-all seed migration is idempotent (re-running lands at same state); locked matrix matches what gadmin captured.
risks: Phase 0 migration is largely UUID swaps + table re-key; risk is missing a SQL constant or test fixture and silently breaking. Mitigated by full test suite gate + smoke nav per protected account.
Phase 0.5 mounting RequirePageAccess on 19 route groups is mechanical but error-prone; a single bad mount could 403 a page that should be public. Mitigated by smoke testing each gated route.
Phase 0.5 deleting 20 legacy /workspace-settings/** pages is destructive; risk is a live link still pointing to one. Mitigated by grepping for those routes pre-delete + 410-Gone handlers if needed.

Phase 0.5 the 1s in-process version cache means a 1-second window where a revocation hasn't propagated cluster-wide. Acceptable for current single-instance dev; not for prod. Mitigated by TD-PG-003 (LISTEN/NOTIFY) for multi-instance.

Phase 1 avatar-bucket exclusion from the grid combined with server-side enforcement means there's no UI for editing avatar grants ever. If you ever need to (e.g. revoke a corrupted grant), you'll need DB-direct or a one-off admin tool. Acceptable invariant.
Phase 1 audit logging volume could be high if gadmin clicks a lot. Mitigated by per-write entries (no batching). Re-evaluate if log volume becomes a problem.
Phase 1.5 polling adds load to /api/nav/catalogue endpoint. Mitigated by tab-focus check + client-side version comparison (no full re-render unless version changed).
Phase 2 capture-then-lock has a manual step (gadmin signals 'done editing'). Mitigated by treating Phase 2 as an explicit on-demand commit rather than CI-automated.

Cross-cutting routes (/nav, /workspaces, /me, /errors, /samantha/v2/search, /rank, /custom-pages) are not gated by RequirePageAccess in Phase 0.5. They remain on RequireAuth + per-route RequirePermission. TD-PG-001 covers per-resource decisions.

Existing PLA-0007 exempt list (dev/registries/role_literals_exempt.json) shrinks but does not reach zero in this plan. Several files listed there no longer exist in the production tree (already migrated and deleted) — exempt list housekeeping is a side task.

Renaming permission codes invalidates any stored permission-code grants in /admin/roles UI state for tenants — but the grant is keyed by permission_id (UUID), not code, so no data loss. UI labels update.
user_role Postgres enum is left in place (deferred Migration Z per existing PLA-0007 plan). No conflict; users.role enum column still works as backwards-compat read.

backend/internal/auth/middleware.go — RequireAuth + RequirePermission patterns to extend

Audit findings (4 parallel Sonnet agents, 2026-05-14): UUID retirement = 79 lines / 17 files; pages = 34 mapped / 41 unmapped / 5 dynamic; route groups = 31 (19 gateable); role gates = 12 production sites + 5 type-narrowing arms
